title: The Overwhelming Case for No First Use
summary: The arguments in favor of the United States' declaring that the only purpose of its nuclear weapons is to deter others who possess them from using theirs — in other words, that in no circumstances will this country use nuclear weapons first — are far stronger than the arguments against this stance. It must be hoped that the next US administration will take this no-first-use step promptly.
body: Abstract The arguments in favor of the United States' declaring that the only purpose of its nuclear weapons is to deter others who possess them from using theirs — in other words, that in no circumstances will this country use nuclear weapons first — are far stronger than the arguments against this stance. It must be hoped that the next US administration will take this no-first-use step promptly. The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation Holdren, John P. "The Overwhelming Case for No First Use." Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists , vol. 76. no. 1. (January 13, 2020): 3–7 .
